title: Houthis Launch Third Coordinated Ballistic Missile Barrage at Israel Since Entering Iran-Israel War
detail: Yemen's Houthi forces launched their third coordinated ballistic missile attack on Israel on April 1, 2026, claiming the operation was conducted jointly with Iran and Hezbollah and targeting what they described as 'sensitive Israeli enemy targets.' The Houthis stated the barrage was part of their declared participation in the wider Iran-Israel war that began in late March 2026. Israel's military confirmed its air defenses responded to a missile threat from Yemen; the missile was intercepted with no casualties or damage reported. The Houthis had resumed attacks on Israel beginning March 30 — the first since the Gaza ceasefire took effect in October 2025 — and fired for a third consecutive time on April 1. The coordinated claims with Iran and Hezbollah signal a deliberate Axis of Resistance messaging campaign timed to Passover (which began April 1), aimed at maximizing psychological pressure on Israel as US-mediated Gaza diplomacy remains stalled and Phase 2 negotiations are suspended. US and Israeli analysts described the attack as primarily symbolic, with Israeli air defenses performing as designed.

media.caption: Israel's Iron Dome air defense system intercepts a Houthi ballistic missile over central Israel during Passover
Houthi military spokesman Yahya Sarea announces coordinated missile operation targeting Israel

title: UN OCHA Warns Gaza Faces Return to Famine by Mid-April as All Land Crossings Remain Closed
detail: As April 1, 2026 — Passover — arrived, the Gaza Strip remained under a complete land aid blockade that UN OCHA warned could tip the territory back into full famine conditions by mid-April. Israel has kept all crossings closed since February 28, when the Iran-Israel war began. Kerem Shalom has been the sole cargo crossing with extremely limited throughput, and UNDP shipments remain suspended following the March 25 contraband discovery. The 2026 Flash Appeal for Gaza ($4.1 billion) stands over 93% unfunded. UNICEF reported over 350,000 children with acute malnutrition. More than 46% of essential medicines are out of stock; 66% of surgical consumables are depleted. The ICJ's March 22 order — directing Israel to ensure basic needs are met and support UNRWA operations — has not been implemented. Thirty-seven humanitarian organizations, including MSF, IRC, Oxfam, and NRC, remain banned from operations in Gaza. Lazzarini (UNRWA) reiterated that 391 UNRWA staff have been killed, and called again for a formal UN investigation.

media.caption: Gaza faces return to famine as food prices soar and all land crossings remain closed
